content:
🌟 netwatch | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Live network forensics and incident response in the terminal.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time network forensics TUI • TLS 1.3 decryption • JA4 / JA4Q fingerprinting • 17 L7 protocol decoders • Kernel-level process attribution (eBPF)
📖 Summary: NetWatch is a real-time network forensics tool that runs in the terminal, offering deep packet inspection, TLS 1.3 decryption, and network intelligence like JA4 fingerprinting and C2 beaconing detection. It provides live triage and evidence capture capabilities without requiring any configuration.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ VidBee | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Downloading videos and audio from a wide variety of online sources.
✨ Key Features: • Download videos and audios from 1000+ websites worldwide • Built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp • Clean, intuitive user interface • One-click pause/resume/retry • Real-time progress tracking
📖 Summary: VidBee is an open-source video downloader built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp. It allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites with a clean, intuitive interface and features like RSS auto-download.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ ai-website-cloner-template | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automatically reverse-engineer and clone existing websites into a functional Next.js project using AI.
✨ Key Features: • Clone any website with one command • AI-powered website reconstruction • Extracts design tokens and assets • Writes component specifications • Dispatches parallel builders for section reconstruction
📖 Summary: This repository provides a reusable template for cloning any website into a Next.js codebase using AI coding agents. It automates the process of inspecting a site, extracting design elements, and reconstructing its structure.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 marketingskills | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To equip AI coding agents with specialized marketing knowledge and workflows, allowing them to assist users with a wide range of marketing tasks.
✨ Key Features: • AI agent skills for marketing tasks • Supports conversion optimization, copywriting, SEO, analytics, growth engineering
📖 Summary: This repository provides a collection of specialized marketing skills for AI agents, enabling them to perform tasks like conversion optimization, copywriting, SEO, analytics, and growth engineering. It's designed for technical marketers and founders to enhance AI coding agent capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 ml-intern | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To act as an autonomous ML engineer, automating ML development tasks from research to deployment using the Hugging Face ecosystem.
✨ Key Features: • Autonomous ML code research, writing, and shipping • Integration with Hugging Face ecosystem (docs, papers, datasets, compute) • Interactive and headless CLI modes • Support for various LLMs including local models via LiteLLM • Optional sandbox environment for tool execution
📖 Summary: ml-intern is an open-source AI agent that automates the process of researching, writing, and shipping ML code. It leverages the Hugging Face ecosystem for access to documentation, papers, datasets, and cloud compute, offering both interactive and headless modes for various use cases.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
